Party_Details.Name: Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Ngu Tieng Ung was appointed as a director of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd
Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, Creanovate Pte Ltd, and Tang entered into a Joint Venture Agreement for coal trading
Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced $940,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd
Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, Creanovate Pte Ltd, and PT PAC entered into a Subscription Agreement
Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced a total of $3.26m to Creanovate Pte Ltd

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the defendant company is legally responsible to refund the advances made under the Subscription Agreement due to non-fulfillment of conditions precedent
Party_Details.Rule: A company must refund advances if conditions precedent in a contract are not fulfilled by the agreed deadline
Party_Details.Application: The conditions precedent were not met, requiring a refund of the advances. the Settlement Agreement superseded the Subscription Agreement, negating the refund obligation
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the conditions precedent were not fulfilled, and the Settlement Agreement did not exempt Creanovate from refunding the advances.

Party_Details.Name: Creanovate Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Tang Kok Heng was the majority and controlling shareholder of Creanovate Pte Ltd
Creanovate Pte Ltd entered into a Joint Venture Agreement with Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd and Tang for coal trading
Creanovate Pte Ltd received an advance of $940,000 from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd
Creanovate Pte Ltd entered into a Subscription Agreement with Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd and PT PAC
Creanovate Pte Ltd received a total of $3.26m in advances from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the company is legally responsible to refund the advances due to the non-fulfillment of conditions precedent in the Subscription Agreement
Party_Details.Rule: A company is obligated to refund advances if conditions precedent in a contract are not fulfilled by the agreed deadline
Party_Details.Application: The Settlement Agreement superseded the Subscription Agreement, negating the refund obligation. the conditions precedent were not met, requiring a refund of the advances
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the conditions precedent were not fulfilled, and the Settlement Agreement did not exempt Creanovate from refunding the advances.

Party_Details.Name: Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Ngu Tieng Ung was appointed as a director of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd
Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, Creanovate Pte Ltd, and Tang entered into a Joint Venture Agreement for coal trading
Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced $1m to Creanovate Pte Ltd
Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, Creanovate Pte Ltd, and PT PAC entered into a Subscription Agreement
Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced a total of $3.26m to Creanovate Pte Ltd

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the directors are legally responsible for the unauthorized advances made to the defendant company
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must ensure that company funds are advanced in compliance with statutory provisions and company interests
Party_Details.Application: The advances were unauthorized and contravened statutory provisions. the advances were legitimate business transactions
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the advances were unauthorized and contravened statutory provisions, holding the directors accountable.

Party_Details.Name: Ngu Tieng Ung

Facts.Text: Ngu Tieng Ung was appointed as a director of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd
Ngu Tieng Ung was involved in the Joint Venture Agreement between Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, Creanovate Pte Ltd, and Tang for coal trading
Together with Tang Kok Heng, Ngu Tieng Ung requested a $1m advance for the coal mining business in Indonesia, and the plaintiff advanced that sum to Creanovate on 2005-02-21
Ngu Tieng Ung was involved in the Subscription Agreement between Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, Creanovate Pte Ltd, and PT PAC
Ngu Tieng Ung was a director of the plaintiff when the plaintiff advanced a further $710,000 to Creanovate on 2005-02-03, bringing the total advances to $3.26m

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the director is legally responsible for the unauthorized advances made to the defendant company
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must ensure that company funds are advanced in compliance with statutory provisions and company interests
Party_Details.Application: The advances were unauthorized and contravened statutory provisions. the advances were legitimate business transactions
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the advances were unauthorized and contravened statutory provisions, holding the director accountable.
